Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "damage" or "insurance" (or similar) to find the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage correspondence. Expected findings: Emeka Diallo's formal damage report about the robotic arm prototype cracked during transport by Heartland Movers, Blessing Okafor signing off without verifying foam crating, the delivery receipt signed by an intern, insurance coverage capped at $50,000 against a $90,000 claim leaving a $40,000 gap, and any updates on whether the insurance resolution has moved forward.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Heartland" or "overbilling" or "vendor dispute" or "invoice" (or similar) to find the Heartland Movers Q1 billing dispute. Expected findings: Chloe Vance identified a $12,800 Q1 bill covering 8 moves where only 5 were legitimate (2 cancelled for Marchetti and Osei, 1 was actually Swift's for Venkatesh), putting approximately $4,800 in dispute. Marcus Thorne placed the payment on hold and sent the formal dispute letter to Heartland.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "placarding" (or similar) to find the DOT compliance inquiry and response details. Expected findings: formal inquiry from DOT inspector Linda Castellano (PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), Marcus Thorne's extension request, the revised deadline of June 5 2026, 4 shipments in scope (Mei-Lin Zhao lithium batteries, Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, Raj Patel UPS batteries, Joaquin Reyes solar rig), and the critical gap where Blessing never filed the placarding certificate for the Mosaic Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment exposing MoveOps to $50,000 or more in potential penalties.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or "board" (or similar) to find the Vectral Q3 renewal negotiation history. Expected findings: Rachel Whitfield's demand for a 15% discount citing ExpenseBot issues and onboarding delays, Mina Hashimoto's counter-offer of 10% via the Preferred Partner Program with service enhancements (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews) plus a 12-month extension and 8-relocation minimum. The board was scheduled to decide around April 23. Check whether any response from Rachel came back after the board meeting.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Elena" or "directive" or "Rostova" (or similar) and search Slack using conversations_search_messages in the #executive channel (C004) to identify directives Elena issued in recent weeks and whether follow-ups exist. Expected findings: Elena's directive to pause the ExpenseBot rollout after the pilot accuracy issues, her approval of the Vectral 10% counter-offer terms (relocation services only), her assignment of the Terraform/Vantage account to Mina Hashimoto with an order for Catalina Dubois to send an apology for the reply-all pricing leak, her mandate for an infrastructure cost estimator tool before the next enterprise pitch, her NorthWind containment directive to handle it within the team without an account reshuffle, her vendor strategy memo directive to Marcus Thorne due Wednesday covering renegotiation options, and her stipend verification mandate requiring manual receipt verification for all reimbursement requests over $100. For each directive, the agent should check whether a visible follow-up or completion signal exists in email or Slack.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Mosaic", "Heartland", "DOT", "Vectral", "damage", "vendor", "stipend", "ExpenseBot" (or similar) across channels including #operations (C006), #finance (C005), #engineering (C003), #customer-engagement (C002), and #executive (C004) to find internal context on all open risk items. Expected findings include discussions about the Mosaic prototype damage and vendor accountability, Heartland overbilling dispute details, DOT compliance audit coordination by Chloe Vance, Vectral renewal strategy discussions, stipend fraud findings from Dmitri Volkov's audit (duplicate receipt hashes matching Kevin Tran at Vectral and Andre Washington at Mosaic for identical $89 TechComfort ErgoKey Pro keyboards, plus Jordan Ekwueme at BrightLoop flagged for a similar TechComfort Supply pattern), ExpenseBot pilot issues (83% accuracy, false rejections at Vectral, false approvals at Mosaic), and any other risk signals such as the Terraform reply-all pricing leak and the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident.
OE 7: Search CRM for deals and company records using crm_search_deals and crm_search_companies with queries related to "Vectral", "Mosaic", and other active client names (or similar). Expected findings: Vectral Q3 renewal deal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk) at "at_risk" stage with no confirmed board outcome, Mosaic Robotics Zara Okoye relocation deal flagged with damage claim risk at $2,700, the Terraform/Vantage enterprise deal at $1,850,000 (with the reply-all pricing leak engagement note), and other active deals across Canopy Health, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, BrightLoop Analytics, NorthWind Technologies, Sunbelt Venture Partners, and Palmetto Community Foundation with their current stages and amounts.
OE 8: List CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements for Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic), Vectral Systems (company_vectral), and other active clients to retrieve engagement history with risk-relevant notes. Expected findings: the Mosaic damage incident engagement detailing the $90,000 prototype value and $40,000 insurance gap with the account flagged at risk, the Vectral renewal negotiation engagement with the full counter-offer terms and board timeline, and engagement notes for other clients showing any at-risk signals including the Terraform reply-all incident and the Canopy Health executive relocation.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for invoices and bills using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills to compile all open financial exposures with actual dollar amounts. Expected findings: Heartland Movers Q1 bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 at $12,800 with a hold version (HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD), pending liability accrual ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC at $90,000 for the damaged prototype labeled as a contingent liability, DOT penalty accrual ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 at $50,000 due June 5, Mosaic credit memo CM-2026-0415 at negative $90,000, KeyMove Specialty Transport bill KM-44192-ICR at $1,200 for an Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch insurance claim rider during stairwell extraction, and stipend-related bills flagged in the fraud audit: STIP-2026-0089 for Kevin Tran at $89 (already auto-approved and paid before the duplicate was discovered), STIP-2026-0094 for Andre Washington at $89 with admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne due to duplicate receipt hash flag, and STIP-2026-0097 for Jordan Ekwueme at $45 with admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne due to PDF template match flag.
OE 10: Search Linear for existing tracking tickets using linear_list_issues and linear_list_projects with queries related to "Heartland", "DOT", "Mosaic", "compliance", "vendor", "stipend", "audit", "Sunbelt", "Palmetto", "NorthWind", "Vectral" (or similar). Expected findings: issue_heartland_q1_recon (vendor invoice reconciliation, P2), project_dot_compliance_audit (DOT Compliance Audit started, led by Chloe Vance) with sub-tickets including issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic for the missing Tanaka placarding certificate at P1 and issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, and issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack for the other three in-scope shipments, issue_audit_expenses (full audit of 47 auto-processed pilot expenses, Urgent), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (receipt hash fingerprinting, P1), ExpenseBot remediation tickets including issue_p1_mosaic_malformed and issue_p1_vectral_config for client policy configuration fixes, the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130, P1), and the NorthWind retention response plan ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, P1). The agent should note which risks already have tickets and which do not so it knows what new tickets need to be created.
OE 11: Search Airtable relocation records and stipend transaction records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, tables: Relocations and Stipend Transactions) for Mosaic, Vectral, and other at-risk client records to check operational status. Expected findings include active Mosaic relocations (Zara Okoye completed with damage, Deshawn Hartley with April 19 trade show deadline, Keiko Tanaka in progress), active Vectral relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya in progress), and stipend transaction records showing flagged or held entries from the ExpenseBot audit.
OE 12: Look up contact details for Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, Alejandro Fuentes, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts to retrieve their email addresses before sending the briefing email and creating the calendar event. Expected: elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 13: Look up the Slack channel IDs for #operations and #finance using channels_list. Expected: C006 for #operations, C005 for #finance.
OE 14: Send a structured briefing email using send_email from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com covering each open financial exposure with the dollar figure, current status, and who is responsible. Key items to include: (a) Mosaic prototype damage at $90,000 with a $40,000 insurance gap and the current claim status, owned by Emeka Diallo; (b) Heartland Movers Q1 overbilling dispute at approximately $4,800 contested out of $12,800 total, payment on hold, owned by Marcus Thorne; (c) DOT/PHMSA compliance penalties at $50,000 or more in exposure with a June 5 deadline and the missing placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka shipment, led by Chloe Vance; (d) Vectral Q3 renewal at risk with the board decision pending from April 23, managed by Mina Hashimoto; (e) any additional exposures discovered such as the suspicious stipend submissions across three clients, the ExpenseBot pilot financial impact, and any other open disputes or liabilities found in the financial records.
OE 15: Create engagement notes in the CRM using crm_create_engagement for accounts where the risk picture has changed. At minimum, create notes for Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic) documenting the current damage claim and insurance status with dollar amounts, and for Vectral Systems (company_vectral) noting the pending board outcome and open renewal risk. Additional engagement notes should be created for any other accounts where the investigation revealed risk changes.
OE 16: Create Linear tickets using linear_create_issue for any open risks that do not already have tracking tickets. The agent should cross-reference findings from the Linear search against the full list of discovered exposures and create tickets for untracked items. Assign each ticket to the appropriate team (team_operations for vendor and compliance issues, team_engineering for system and tool issues). Priority should reflect the urgency of each risk.
OE 17: Schedule a 45-minute calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for Tuesday April 28 2026 in the afternoon, with elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com as attendees. The title should relate to "Financial Risk Review Prep" or similar.
OE 18: Post a message in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message asking Chloe Vance for a current status update on the DOT compliance response and mentioning that Elena will be asking about it on Wednesday.
OE 19: Post a message in #finance (channel C005) using conversations_add_message asking Marcus Thorne for current status on the Heartland vendor dispute and any other open vendor payment issues, and mentioning that Elena will be asking about these on Wednesday.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Mosaic" or "prototype" or "damage" or "insurance" (or similar) to find the Mosaic Robotics prototype damage correspondence. Expected findings: Emeka Diallo's formal damage report about the robotic arm prototype cracked during transport by Heartland Movers, Blessing Okafor signing off without verifying foam crating, the delivery receipt signed by an intern, insurance coverage capped at $50,000 against a $90,000 claim leaving a $40,000 gap, and any updates on whether the insurance resolution has moved forward.
OE 2: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Heartland" or "overbilling" or "vendor dispute" or "invoice" (or similar) to find the Heartland Movers Q1 billing dispute. Expected findings: Chloe Vance identified a $12,800 Q1 bill covering 8 moves where only 5 were legitimate (2 cancelled for Marchetti and Osei, 1 was actually Swift's for Venkatesh), putting approximately $4,800 in dispute. Marcus Thorne placed the payment on hold and sent the formal dispute letter to Heartland.
OE 3: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "DOT" or "PHMSA" or "compliance" or "hazmat" or "placarding" (or similar) to find the DOT compliance inquiry and response details. Expected findings: formal inquiry from DOT inspector Linda Castellano (PHMSA Case No. PHMSA-2026-04-1187), Marcus Thorne's extension request, the revised deadline of June 5 2026, 4 shipments in scope (Mei-Lin Zhao lithium batteries, Keiko Tanaka laser cutter, Raj Patel UPS batteries, Joaquin Reyes solar rig), and the critical gap where Blessing never filed the placarding certificate for the Mosaic Keiko Tanaka laser cutter shipment exposing MoveOps to $50,000 or more in potential penalties.
OE 4: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Vectral" or "Rachel Whitfield" or "renewal" or "board" (or similar) to find the Vectral Q3 renewal negotiation history. Expected findings: Rachel Whitfield's demand for a 15% discount citing ExpenseBot issues and onboarding delays, Mina Hashimoto's counter-offer of 10% via the Preferred Partner Program with service enhancements (dedicated onboarding coordinator, 48-hour response SLA, quarterly account reviews) plus a 12-month extension and 8-relocation minimum. The board was scheduled to decide around April 23. Check whether any response from Rachel came back after the board meeting.
OE 5: Search emails using search_emails with queries related to "Elena" or "directive" or "Rostova" (or similar) and search Slack using conversations_search_messages in the #executive channel (C004) to identify directives Elena issued in recent weeks and whether follow-ups exist. Expected findings: Elena's directive to pause the ExpenseBot rollout after the pilot accuracy issues, her approval of the Vectral 10% counter-offer terms (relocation services only), her assignment of the Terraform/Vantage account to Mina Hashimoto with an order for Catalina Dubois to send an apology for the reply-all pricing leak, her mandate for an infrastructure cost estimator tool before the next enterprise pitch, her NorthWind containment directive to handle it within the team without an account reshuffle, her vendor strategy memo directive to Marcus Thorne due Wednesday covering renegotiation options, and her stipend verification mandate requiring manual receipt verification for all reimbursement requests over $100. For each directive, the agent should check whether a visible follow-up or completion signal exists in email or Slack.
OE 6: Search Slack using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "Mosaic", "Heartland", "DOT", "Vectral", "damage", "vendor", "stipend", "ExpenseBot" (or similar) across channels including #operations (C006), #finance (C005), #engineering (C003), #customer-engagement (C002), and #executive (C004) to find internal context on all open risk items. Expected findings include discussions about the Mosaic prototype damage and vendor accountability, Heartland overbilling dispute details, DOT compliance audit coordination by Chloe Vance, Vectral renewal strategy discussions, stipend fraud findings from Dmitri Volkov's audit (duplicate receipt hashes matching Kevin Tran at Vectral and Andre Washington at Mosaic for identical $89 TechComfort ErgoKey Pro keyboards, plus Jordan Ekwueme at BrightLoop flagged for a similar TechComfort Supply pattern), ExpenseBot pilot issues (83% accuracy, false rejections at Vectral, false approvals at Mosaic), and any other risk signals such as the Terraform reply-all pricing leak and the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap incident.
OE 7: Search CRM for deals and company records using crm_search_deals and crm_search_companies with queries related to "Vectral", "Mosaic", and other active client names (or similar). Expected findings: Vectral Q3 renewal deal (deal_vectral_q3_renewal_risk) at "at_risk" stage with no confirmed board outcome, Mosaic Robotics Zara Okoye relocation deal flagged with damage claim risk at $2,700, the Terraform/Vantage enterprise deal at $1,850,000 (with the reply-all pricing leak engagement note), and other active deals across Canopy Health, GreenStack Energy, PivotPoint Labs, BrightLoop Analytics, NorthWind Technologies, Sunbelt Venture Partners, and Palmetto Community Foundation with their current stages and amounts.
OE 8: List CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements for Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic), Vectral Systems (company_vectral), and other active clients to retrieve engagement history with risk-relevant notes. Expected findings: the Mosaic damage incident engagement detailing the $90,000 prototype value and $40,000 insurance gap with the account flagged at risk, the Vectral renewal negotiation engagement with the full counter-offer terms and board timeline, and engagement notes for other clients showing any at-risk signals including the Terraform reply-all incident and the Canopy Health executive relocation.
OE 9: Search QuickBooks for invoices and bills using quickbooks_search_invoices and quickbooks_search_bills to compile all open financial exposures with actual dollar amounts. Expected findings: Heartland Movers Q1 bill HM-2026-Q1-0042 at $12,800 with a hold version (HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD), pending liability accrual ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC at $90,000 for the damaged prototype labeled as a contingent liability, DOT penalty accrual ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 at $50,000 due June 5, Mosaic credit memo CM-2026-0415 at negative $90,000, KeyMove Specialty Transport bill KM-44192-ICR at $1,200 for an Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch insurance claim rider during stairwell extraction, and stipend-related bills flagged in the fraud audit: STIP-2026-0089 for Kevin Tran at $89 (already auto-approved and paid before the duplicate was discovered), STIP-2026-0094 for Andre Washington at $89 with admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne due to duplicate receipt hash flag, and STIP-2026-0097 for Jordan Ekwueme at $45 with admin hold placed by Marcus Thorne due to PDF template match flag.
OE 10: Search Linear for existing tracking tickets using linear_list_issues and linear_list_projects with queries related to "Heartland", "DOT", "Mosaic", "compliance", "vendor", "stipend", "audit", "Sunbelt", "Palmetto", "NorthWind", "Vectral" (or similar). Expected findings: issue_heartland_q1_recon (vendor invoice reconciliation, P2), project_dot_compliance_audit (DOT Compliance Audit started, led by Chloe Vance) with sub-tickets including issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic for the missing Tanaka placarding certificate at P1 and issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack, issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, and issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack for the other three in-scope shipments, issue_audit_expenses (full audit of 47 auto-processed pilot expenses, Urgent), issue_stipend_duplicate_detection (receipt hash fingerprinting, P1), ExpenseBot remediation tickets including issue_p1_mosaic_malformed and issue_p1_vectral_config for client policy configuration fixes, the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice reconstruction ticket (linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130, P1), and the NorthWind retention response plan ticket (linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1, P1). The agent should note which risks already have tickets and which do not so it knows what new tickets need to be created.
OE 11: Search Airtable relocation records and stipend transaction records using airtable_list_records or airtable_search_records (base: appMoveOpsOps001, tables: Relocations and Stipend Transactions) for Mosaic, Vectral, and other at-risk client records to check operational status. Expected findings include active Mosaic relocations (Zara Okoye completed with damage, Deshawn Hartley with April 19 trade show deadline, Keiko Tanaka in progress), active Vectral relocations (Priya Suresh, Terrence Nakamura, Camille Oduya in progress), and stipend transaction records showing flagged or held entries from the ExpenseBot audit.
OE 12: Look up contact details for Elena Rostova, Marcus Thorne, Alejandro Fuentes, and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts to retrieve their email addresses before sending the briefing email and creating the calendar event. Expected: elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com.
OE 13: Look up the Slack channel IDs for #operations and #finance using channels_list. Expected: C006 for #operations, C005 for #finance.
OE 14: Send a structured briefing email using send_email from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to elena.rostova@moveops.com covering each open financial exposure with the dollar figure, current status, and who is responsible. Key items to include: (a) Mosaic prototype damage at $90,000 with a $40,000 insurance gap and the current claim status, owned by Emeka Diallo; (b) Heartland Movers Q1 overbilling dispute at approximately $4,800 contested out of $12,800 total, payment on hold, owned by Marcus Thorne; (c) DOT/PHMSA compliance penalties at $50,000 or more in exposure with a June 5 deadline and the missing placarding certificate for the Keiko Tanaka shipment, led by Chloe Vance; (d) Vectral Q3 renewal at risk with the board decision pending from April 23, managed by Mina Hashimoto; (e) any additional exposures discovered such as the suspicious stipend submissions across three clients, the ExpenseBot pilot financial impact, and any other open disputes or liabilities found in the financial records.
OE 15: Create engagement notes in the CRM using crm_create_engagement for accounts where the risk picture has changed. At minimum, create notes for Mosaic Robotics (company_mosaic) documenting the current damage claim and insurance status with dollar amounts, and for Vectral Systems (company_vectral) noting the pending board outcome and open renewal risk. Additional engagement notes should be created for any other accounts where the investigation revealed risk changes.
OE 16: Create Linear tickets using linear_create_issue for any open risks that do not already have tracking tickets. The agent should cross-reference findings from the Linear search against the full list of discovered exposures and create tickets for untracked items. Assign each ticket to the appropriate team (team_operations for vendor and compliance issues, team_engineering for system and tool issues). Priority should reflect the urgency of each risk.
OE 17: Schedule a 45-minute calendar event using calendar_add_calendar_event for Tuesday April 28 2026 in the afternoon, with elena.rostova@moveops.com, marcus.thorne@moveops.com, and alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com as attendees. The title should relate to "Financial Risk Review Prep" or similar.
OE 18: Post a message in #operations (channel C006) using conversations_add_message asking Chloe Vance for a current status update on the DOT compliance response and mentioning that Elena will be asking about it on Wednesday.
OE 19: Post a message in #finance (channel C005) using conversations_add_message asking Marcus Thorne for current status on the Heartland vendor dispute and any other open vendor payment issues, and mentioning that Elena will be asking about these on Wednesday.

25 The model must use a CRM tool (e.g., crm_search_deals, crm_search_companies, or crm_list_engagements) to look up client account, deal, or engagement information.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1Multiple CRM tool calls were made: crm_search_deals (multiple queries), crm_search_companies (multiple queries), and crm_list_engagements for Mosaic, Vectral, and Heartland.
✓Run #2The agent used crm_search_deals multiple times (for Mosaic, Vectral, Heartland, Terraform, BrightLoop, GreenStack, Canopy), as well as crm_search_companies and crm_list_engagements, demonstrating use of CRM tools to look up deal and account information.
✓Run #3The agent used crm_search_deals, crm_search_companies, and crm_list_engagements throughout the trajectory to look up deal, company, and engagement information for Mosaic, Vectral, Heartland, BrightLoop, NorthWind, Sunbelt, Palmetto, and others.
✓Run #4The agent made multiple CRM tool calls including crm_search_deals [42, 53], crm_search_companies [55, 56, 57], and contacts_search_contacts calls, demonstrating use of CRM tools to look up deal and account information.
✓Run #5Tool call #3 (crm_show_data) and tool calls #54–57 (crm_create_engagement) confirm CRM tools were used to both look up and update account information. The initial crm_show_data call was used to gather deal, company, and engagement data.
✓Run #6Multiple CRM tools were used: crm_search_deals (multiple calls for Mosaic, Vectral, Heartland, NorthWind, BrightLoop), crm_search_companies, and crm_list_engagements.

tool selection

✓Run #6quickbooks_search_bills (with fetchAll=true) and quickbooks_search_invoices were both called early in the trajectory to look up financial records.
27 The model must use a Linear tool (e.g., linear_list_issues, linear_list_projects, or linear_get_issue) to check for existing tracking tickets.
tool selection
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times (with limit:100, and with specific queries like 'Heartland billing dispute', 'Mosaic damage', 'stipend duplicate', etc.) to check for existing tracking tickets before creating new ones.

✓Run #4The agent called linear_list_issues multiple times [3, 33-39] with various search queries to check for existing tracking tickets before creating new ones.
✓Run #5Tool call #4 (linear_show_data) and tool call #48 (linear_list_users) confirm Linear tools were used to check existing issues and teams before creating new tickets.
✓Run #6linear_list_issues (limit=100) was called to check existing tickets, followed by multiple linear_get_issue calls to examine specific issues before creating new tickets.
28 The model must use an Airtable tool (e.g., airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records) to look up relocation records or stipend transaction records.
tool selection
 ✗✗✗✗✓✗ 17%
✗Run #1Only airtable_list_bases was called — this only lists the available bases/databases, not actual record data. No airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record calls were made to query the Relocations or Stipend Transactions tables in base appMoveOpsOps001.
✗Run #2The agent only called airtable_list_bases (to enumerate available bases) but never called airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record to actually query relocation records or stipend transaction data from the Airtable bases.
✗Run #3The agent called airtable_list_bases and airtable_list_tables but never called airtable_search_records, airtable_list_records, or airtable_get_record to actually query the Relocations or Stipend Transactions tables. The agent listed the tables but did not retrieve any actual records from Airtable.
✗Run #4The agent only called airtable_list_bases [6] to discover available databases, but never called airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records to actually look up relocation records or stipend transaction records in the MoveOps Operations base. The criterion requires querying actual table data.
✓Run #5Tool call #5 (airtable_show_data) confirms an Airtable tool was used to look up records. The DOT compliance Airtable checklist is also referenced in the Slack message to Chloe, confirming operational Airtable data was accessed.
✗Run #6No Airtable tool calls were found in the entire trajectory. The agent did not query Airtable for relocation records or stipend transaction records, missing this data source entirely.
Total 24/2827/2826/2820/2823/2826/28 88%
